item_specifications.special_feature: Easy to Clean, Non Skid Bottom, Orthopedic

about_item: Chew Proof Design: This crate bed has a solid aluminum frame that protects all vulnerable spots from chewing. Practically indestructible, it’s the only crate pad that will hold up against the most persistent or destructive of chewers, especially those that chew heavily from anxiety or boredom.

Durable Ripstop Ballistic Bed Sling: This heavy-duty fabric features a dense weave (1200D) of thick fibers to withstand punctures and tears from scratching, digging, and chewing. It’s also resistant to dirt, fur, odor, and water, making it easy to clean. Can be used in a crate, inside the home, or outside in the yard.

Padded for Extra Comfort: The bed's padded center is filled to a 1.5” thickness with recycled polyester fiberfill for extra softness and insulation. It can be used on the floor of the crate like a regular crate pad, or you can add the included legs to convert it into an elevated bed that will provide orthopedic-style support for your dog’s joints and spine.

Made for Wire Crates: Our Chew Proof Armored Padded Dog Crate Beds are specifically sized for use in standard wire crates. Simply remove the bottom tray from the crate and slide this bed in at a diagonal to install it. Since the center is padded, you can forgo the legs to give your dog more headroom.

Non-skid Feet: The bed features rubber corner pads and the included legs are also capped with soft feet to keep it from sliding around. No matter how you use the bed, your floor will be protected from scratch marks and scuffs.

summary.text: Customers find the dog bed highly durable with zero tears or rips, and appreciate its quality and value as the best investment for their pets. The bed is perfect for aggressive chewers and provides a comfortable place to rest, with customers noting their dogs sleep well on it. Customers praise its cushioning, ease of cleaning, and perfect fit for crates.

authors_reviews.title: Worth Every Cent: Running Edits at Beginning and End of Introduction
authors_reviews.text: Six Month Edit: Absolutely zero chew or scuff marks and punctures on either of the two pads in six months. The inside pad is a little dirty as it is used night and day, on and off, but the one in the outside crate is in as-new condition as it is stored inside the house when not in use on the covered deck. Another customer asked how to launder it if it is in a solid frame and my reply was to just wipe the pad down with a rag. I guess, since the frame was initially screwed together, it could be disassembled too, but that is a lot of work for a product that can be easily cleaned with a washcloth and some elbow grease. Early Edit: Two or so months in and the original pad still looks new, no tears, gashes, or chew marks. I guess the dog recognized he wasn’t going to be able to get a grip on the pad and gave up. I like this pad so much I just ordered a second one of the same size for his larger solid barstock kennel outside on the deck. Even though the deck kennel and stainless steel pan are larger, I wanted the same size pad and frame so I could get it in and out of the crate door quickly in wet weather. He just fits on the 35 by 22 inch size and it is better than the hard steel pan, so good enough is good enough. Bottom Line Edit Up Front: This pad looks brand new after several weeks of daily and nightly use. My problem child has house priviledges, but is crated at night and off and on during the day if I can't keep my eyes on him, so he is in and out of jail a lot. The monster has made no attempt to damage the pad, probably because it does not have the liabilities of all the others, such as chewy seams, tasty corners, and tempting loose threads. I am shocked as he has a mouth the size of an alligator and has to grab everything in his environment with it, chunks of concrete, flower pots, weeds, me, etc. Enough about the dog; I am going to put my money where my review is and buy another for his outside solid bar stock kennel on the deck when the weather gets warmer, with the understanding that it may be chew-proof, but not waterproof, and has to be brought in during storms. Hopefully this is the final edit and nothing changes. Well, it arrived. A great looking product. I wish this pad had shown up in the list of similar products much earlier. Maybe it is new to Amazon. I would have saved bunch of money on other "indestructible" pads. I had to get a solid bar stock jail cell style kennel for my monster, one of the most expensive available, that should show up as a top CRITICAL review under my tag, so click on "all reviews" if interested. After several destroyed pads, the beast was reduced to sleeping on a 36 by 23 inch stainless steel pan intended for under the crate, but placed inside instead, not the most comfortable situation, but the best I could do considering my dog's destructive capabilities. Then I saw this pad. At a true 35 by 22 inches, it fits perfectly inside the existing metal pan for a solid base. My German Shephard mix seems happy so far and has left it alone. The proof of the pudding will be when I leave the house for a few hours and he gets bored. First Week Edit: So far so good. The monster loves the pad as it is cushy and warm. I like it as it is not a cold uninsulated stainless steel sheet pan with an air gap between the bottom kennel slats and the floor. One of my dumb fully-clawed cats ran through the house into the open kennel door, followed immediately by the 85 pound GS mix, so I had a WWE cage match inside a 42 by 30 inch jail cell crate until I could get them both out again. I thought the cat's claws would damage the pad fabric, the dog, and me, but we all eventually came came out fine and the pad still looked brand new. I am impressed so far with the pad, but not so much with the judgement of the cat. Part of the secret to the pad's integrity is there are no loose pieces of fabric material or threads to chew on or flexible corners to get a grip with. The sturdy aluminum frame helps hold it in place and the material is appaprently pretty tough. So far. Oh, in regards to size, the pad I ordered claims 35 by 22 inches and it measures exactly as advertised at the reinforcing plastic corner pieces, which are rounded at the apex, so the pad and frame should fit accurate and true dimensional spaces. Just measure carefully.
authors_reviews.author: 11 Bravo

authors_reviews.title: A year later: the perfect durable, safe, comfortable bed for aggressive chewer!
authors_reviews.text: Over a year ago I purchased this fantastic elevated dog bed for my 75 lb lab mix. My sweet pup has chewed his way through many expensive dog beds with nothing lasting long. He is only in his crate at night so I wanted a bed that was both safe and comfortable, this bed has held up perfectly!!! I will definitely purchase again and highly recommend this bed for comfort and safety. It fits perfectly within his 48” Midwest crate with no wiggle room for claws to get caught, fantastic bed!!!
authors_reviews.author: Carolyn E

authors_reviews.title: Great Buy!
authors_reviews.text: I recently purchased the K9 Ballistics Chew Proof Armored Padded Dog Crate Pad Bed for my active and energetic pup, and I have to say, I'm extremely impressed with this product. This dog bed is designed to withstand even the toughest chewers and provides a comfortable place for my dog to rest.The bed is constructed from a durable and chew-proof material that has stood up to my dog's persistent chewing. The armored material is also waterproof and stain-resistant, which makes it easy to clean and maintain.The bed is also padded for extra comfort and support, which my dog seems to really enjoy. The padding is firm enough to provide support for my dog's joints, but still soft enough for him to feel comfortable.One of the best things about this dog bed is the fact that it is designed to fit perfectly into a dog crate. It comes in a variety of sizes, so it was easy to find the perfect fit for my dog's crate. The bed also has a non-slip bottom, which keeps it securely in place inside the crate.Overall, I would highly recommend the K9 Ballistics Chew Proof Armored Padded Dog Crate Pad Bed to any dog owner who has a chew-happy pup. It's durable, comfortable, and easy to clean, making it a great investment for both you and your furry friend.
authors_reviews.author: Amazon Customer

authors_reviews.title: Expensive but worth it!!
authors_reviews.text: So far so good!!! My pup hasn't figured out a way to destroy this!! Fits in the crate perfectly, nice comfy surface and it is really well made. At least my dog doesn't have a hard surface to lay on because it was coming down to that with all the blankets and stuffed beds she was chewing thru. I was spending so much money finding new replacements all the time!!! This is bed may be a bit pricey but it is money well spent. I think it will last a very long time!!
authors_reviews.author: Dollycat

authors_reviews.title: Sturdy
authors_reviews.text: The bed came as stated. It's been a week so im still waiting to see if it holds up. The large size is perfect, quality is good and the assembly was very easy.
authors_reviews.author: Yaz

product.size: Large (41" L x 27" W x 1" H)

authors_reviews.title: Not indestructible
authors_reviews.text: This held up well initially. My dog scratched a hole in it. Not indestructible apparently.
authors_reviews.author: Ash White

authors_reviews.title: Chew Proof Padded Dog Crate Bed
authors_reviews.text: I have two strong chewers and these padded beds have lived up to their name. These beds are attractive to look at and look good no matter where you place them in your house. They elevate your space over plastic pvc styled beds. I have purchased four of these - two upstairs and two downstairs. The dogs love them as I often find the dogs laying on them instead of the floor or area rug.
authors_reviews.author: bikegirl

product.size: Large (41" L x 27" W x 1" H)

authors_reviews.title: Durability exceeds expectation Worth the Money
authors_reviews.text: I would give it more for durability if I could. I put this in with my bernadoodle. His nails are very strong even when trimmed. He scratches at it almost every day at least once. just superficial marks that don't damage the fabric. Metal rails easy wipe down.He had an accident one night and soaked the entire bed. It didn't leak through. I washed it on gentle and very low to no heat in dryer to make sure fabric doesn't melt or shrink.IMPORTANT!!!! Get the drill bit needed to take it apart. One person can take it apart easily and reassemble it. Easier and faster to reassemble with 2 people though. My husband just happened to have one, but he's a mechanic.
authors_reviews.author: David Stach
